title: arabis drummondii var. oreophila
intro: Arabis drummondii var. oreophila, also known as Drummond's rockcress, is a plant species belonging to the mustard family (Brassicaceae) that is native to North America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Arabis drummondii var. oreophila include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and promote wound healing. Arabis drummondii var. oreophila is often prepared for medicinal use through infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be administered orally or applied topically to the affected area.
